title: Investigate admin deposit page missing records and wrong status display
issue_description: Investigate an issue where the admin deposit page may not be showing all deposits and may not correctly display whether deposits are pending or completed. Systems involved may include AlgonneyPay, treasury events, algonney.com backend, database queries, admin API endpoints, and frontend table/status mapping. Start in investigate mode, read-only only, with one and only one step per cycle. For each step, focus on the single highest-priority check needed to trace the deposit data path end-to-end and determine the exact root cause. Do not change code, schema, or data. Stop only when the issue is proven, blocked, or clearly ready for a fix proposal.
issue_seed_prompt: Investigate admin deposit page missing records and wrong status display

history.step_title: Identify the exact admin deposit data source and status mapping
history.prompt_for_claude: In read-only mode, trace the admin deposit page data source inside algonney.com only. Find: (1) the frontend file/component for the admin deposit page/table, (2) the exact API endpoint it calls, (3) the backend handler/service/query that returns the deposit rows, and (4) the code that maps backend/raw deposit status into the UI label shown in the table. Do not inspect AlgonneyPay or change anything yet. Return only the concrete file paths, function names, endpoint path, and a short summary of the status-mapping logic.
history.claude_output:
SUMMARY:
[1-2 sentence summary of findings]

FILES:
- path: /path/to/file.ext
- function: function_name

FLOW:
1. Step one
2. Step two

FINDINGS:
- Evidence-based finding 1
- Evidence-based finding 2

LIKELY_BREAKPOINTS:
- Potential issue location 1
- Potential issue location 2

RECOMMENDED_NEXT_CHECK:
- Specific next action to verify
history.planner_interpretation: Before checking AlgonneyPay, treasury events, or database rows, we need to prove which algonney.com admin deposit page/API/backend path actually supplies the table and how it maps raw deposit states into the UI labels. This is the single highest-priority check because both missing rows and wrong pending/completed display can originate from the page reading the wrong endpoint, filtering records, or remapping statuses incorrectly.
